All right, we are back. So fulfillment of the actual 21-day reactivation challenge. So now that they have come to Way Enday, they have got their before pictures, they have got into the portal, signed into the portal, where they can access all of the meal plans and everything like that. They've hopefully bought supplements. We're ready to get this party started. So the first thing that we want to do is make sure that they are in the Facebook group so we can build that community. So you'll have that private Facebook group for these challengers and we want to stay super engaged with them. We also want to make sure that they are in the agile campaign. So the agile campaign is a slow-drip campaign that's going to be reaching out to them each week, just seeing how things are going and then finally setting that appointment for Way Outday. But on top of that, you want to make sure that you over deliver with your communication with them. So that means reaching out to them on Wednesdays if they have not attended any classes for the week yet, because chances are they may not come in that week. If they haven't made a workout on Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday, you want to reach out to them and say, hey, how's everything going? Where are you at? You also want to reach out to them based on their results. So make sure that you have a 21-day clipboard just next to your other clipboards that you have, but a 21-day clipboard with your people on there and they're just writing in their results so far. That way you can reach out to them based on how much weight they've lost and you can encourage them. You can give them tips on how to get better results, but you should be tracking their weigh-ins on a weekly basis and their compliance to those weigh-ins and then reaching out, being proactive and reaching out to them. Also be very reactive. So they will most likely be texting you back from the messages that are going out from that agile campaign. So make sure that you are quick on your responses and you get back to them right away. Surprisingly, a lot of members leave gyms because the lack of communication and they don't feel like they're anything special. Now's your chance to prove them that you have really improved in those areas by showing them how well you communicate. I would highly recommend that you assign an accountability coach to oversee this process of the 21 days because these were previous members, which means they're buyers, which means they see the value in what you are doing, but at some point that value went away. But they're at least back again and you have a chance to re-prove it to them. So these may be the easiest members that you can get to sign up when this whole thing is said and done. So really over deliver on the 21 days. You also want to make sure that everybody comes to way out day. So the campaign does give them a link to your calendar so they can schedule themselves, but don't be 100% reliant on that. So make sure you clearly know how many people need to weigh out and then how many people have weighed out in your reach out to everybody, I'm sorry, how many people have scheduled in your reach out to everybody that hasn't scheduled yet and make sure that they get in on the schedule. So you've done that. You do not need to run any special classes for this reactivation play. Whether you're naming it like 21 day holiday hangover, the 21 day bikini summer, whatever you're naming it, you don't need to run special plays for this. I would just encourage maybe having them do some of the workouts that are in the portal that you signed them up for and completing those on maybe the non three days a week that you're at your gym. Have them do those portal workouts. Huge value there. Make sure that on way out day that three things happen. That number one, you are getting their after pictures. So that's going to be huge because you're going to be using those to declare a winner of your challenge. Number two, they are presented an option to sign back up at your gym. So you're going to resell them into a membership, which would be, I think one of the easiest transitions to get them into a membership is an internal play that's starting within your gym. So this 21 day reactivation play, it pairs really well with an internal play. So let's say you have an internal play like big booty boot camp plan to start on as an example, March 1st. That's right around the same time that you want your 21 day challenge to end. That way the conversation is super easy. It's that, you know, when you're on way out day, you can say, Hey, Mrs. Jones, you've made amazing progress during this 21 days. Like you're doing really, really well. We have another challenge that's actually starting next week. And it is for members only, but it's going to be, you'll get this, this, this and this. How about we keep this momentum rocking and we'll use this way out day as your way in for this big booty boot camp. And she's like, Wow, that could be really awesome. And say, Yeah, I mean, you, so you've done three weeks here. The big booty boot camp is four weeks or 28 days or six weeks. However you run it. Let's just keep it rocking. That is for our members only. It does get all of this other stuff, but our memberships are $49 a week. So we'll just keep billing that card $49 a week so you can continue with your membership with us and get right into the big, the big booty boot camp or whatever your internal challenges. You don't even have to come to weigh in day. We'll use this picture that we just took now as your after picture for our 21 day challenge. You just finished, but we'll also use it as your before picture for your new challenge. Oh, and by the way, let's get you re-upped with some supplements or if they haven't bought any, or if they're on auto subscribe, you're fine, but let's get you some new supplements for the next six weeks. You're going to need them. So that's the, that's the second thing you want to make short. That conversation really needs to happen. If you don't have an internal play coming up, just try to get them straight into your EFT, just like you would with a challenger straight into the EFT. And whether it's $49 a week or whatever may be stacked of value on what they're going to be getting, even in addition to what they just got the past 21 days, you'll get to go to these events that we have with our members. You get, and then you just rattle off all the things that you value stack from, and that you would normally talk to a six week challenger at the abandoned hope or that you would normally talk to people about when they finish your challenge. That's why they want to continue. The final thing that you want to make sure happens on way out day is if you can't get them on the EFT, try to get them into a six week challenge. So it's another shot. Yes, you do have to resell them again at the end of the six weeks, but it's better than them walking away empty handed like, Hey, thanks for the 21 days. Try to get them on a deposit where they can apply that towards a membership or when they're cashback, however you sell your six week challenge, try to get them to do that. Right? Then once way in days are concluded and finished, you want to declare a winner of this 21 day challenge. Right? So the way that it's recommended that you do that, and you can do it other ways, but the way that it's recommended is you have all of your before and after pictures that you have just gotten from this 21 days. You and your team go through those and you identify the top 10, the top 10 before and after pictures that you think are just stellar and maybe they can write a little whatever or maybe have some in body sheets, but pick your top 10 people that absolutely crushed it. Get those top 10 and create a Facebook poll in your members group and put those top 10 pictures in there and have your members decide on who they think had the best transformation and they're voting on people that are not members. So they shouldn't be partial to anyone or they shouldn't be biased. They're just looking at the pictures and saying, wow, that lady crushed it. Right? And I think that the people that are being voted on will really appreciate that because it's not like your training staff has like, well, this person has been in every class. I really like them. We're going to vote on them. You know, it's completely a fair way to judge who the winner is and then give the winner the, you know, go on Facebook Live or make a really good post or announcement and let, like, declare who the winner is and give them the prize, which we recommend would be like maybe a four pack from Prestige Labs or an ultimate pack or maybe a four pack plus a six week challenge, whatever you decide that your prizes for anyone that completes it or that gets voted on as the best, make sure you make a big deal of it and, you know, over deliver with the value and all of that stuff. So that's how you fulfill this challenge. The main point is to get them with amazing results to weigh out day. So you have all these testimonials, you vote on the winner and you upsell them or resell them into either an internal challenge straight to your EFT or last case scenario, you sell them into a six week challenge that they can put a deposit down and win it back or apply towards membership. So that's how the fulfillment goes and hopefully you guys are able to execute this play and just absolutely crush it.
